revision.text:
<!-- 削除についての議論が終了するまで、下記のメッセージ部分は除去しないでください。もしあなたがこのテンプレートを除去した場合、差し戻されます。またページが保護されることもあります。 --> {{Sakujo/本体|2022年6月15日|2005年12月28日に作成された日蓮宗の僧の記事}} <!-- 削除についての議論が終了するまで、上記部分は削除しないでください。 --> {{Copyrights}} [[File:Nose Myokenzan Nikken.jpg|thumb|200px|right|日乾像(能勢妙見山)]] '''日乾'''（にっけん、[[永禄]]3年（[[1560年]]）- [[寛永]]12年[[10月27日 (旧暦)|10月27日]]（[[1635年]][[12月6日]]））は、[[安土桃山時代]]から[[江戸時代]]前期にかけての[[日蓮宗]]の[[僧]]。[[若狭国]]の出身。字は孝順。寂照院と号した。[[紀伊国]][[紀州藩]]主[[徳川頼宣]]の帰依を受け、頼宣の援助により[[駿河国]]蓮永寺を再興している。
==関連項目== *[[佐渡始顕本尊]]

revision.text:
{{Cite needs improvement|date=December 21, 2022, 10:46 (UTC)}}
[[File:Nose Myokenzan Nikken.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Statue of Nikken at Nose Myokenzan]]
'''Nikken''' (日乾, 1560 - December 6, 1635) was a Buddhist monk of the [[Nichiren-shū]] sect active during the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]] to the early [[Edo period]]. He was born in [[Wakasa Province]] and had the Dharma name "Kōjun" (孝順). He also bore the title "Jakushō-in" (寂照院). Nikken gained the patronage of [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the lord of [[Kii Province]]'s [[Kishū Domain]]. With Yorinobu's support, Nikken played a significant role in the revival of [[Rennyō-ji Temple]] (蓮永寺) in [[Suruga Province]].
== Related Topics == * [[Jōshō-ji Temple (Kyoto)]] * [[Sadoga Hajime Hokke Honzon]] (佐渡始顕本尊)

[[File:Nose Myokenzan Nikken.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Statue of Nikken at Nose Myokenzan]]
'''Nikken''' (日乾, 1560 - December 6, 1635) was a Buddhist monk of the [[Nichiren-shū]] sect active during the [[Azuchi-Momoyama period]] to the early [[Edo period]]. He was born in [[Wakasa Province]] and had the Dharma name "Kōjun" (孝順). He also bore the title "Jakushō-in" (寂照院). Nikken gained the patronage of [[Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the lord of [[Kii Province]]'s [[Kishū Domain]]. With Yorinobu's support, Nikken played a significant role in the revival of [[Rennyō-ji Temple]] (蓮永寺) in [[Suruga Province]]. == See Also == * [[Jōshō-ji Temple (Kyoto)]] * [[Sadoga Hajime Hokke Honzon]] (佐渡始顕本尊)

{{Infobox person|image=File:Nose Myokenzan Nikken.jpg|birth_date=1560|death_date={{death date|1635|12|6}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|qid=Q205662}}|religion={{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|qid=Q871371}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11504819]]
Wikidata name: Nikken
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Unreferenceds improvement|date=December 21, 2022, 10:46 (UTC)}}

[[File:Nose Myokenzan Nikken.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Statue of Nikken at Nose Myokenzan]]

'''Nikken''' (日乾, 1560 - December 6, 1635) was a Buddhist monk of the [[:en:Nichiren-shū|Nichiren-shū]] sect active during the [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Azuchi–Momoyama period]] to the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. He was born in [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]] and had the Dharma name "Kōjun" (孝順). He also bore the title "Jakushō-in" (寂照院). Nikken gained the patronage of [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the lord of [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]]'s [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]]. He was born in [[:en:Wakasa Province|Wakasa Province]] and had the Dharma name "Kōjun" (孝順). He also bore the title "Jakushō-in" (寂照院). Nikken gained the patronage of [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], the lord of [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]]'s [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]]. With Yorinobu's support, Nikken played a significant role in the revival of [[Rennyō-ji Temple]] (蓮永寺) in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]]. == See Also == * [[Jōshō-ji Temple (Kyoto)]] * [[Sadoga Hajime Hokke Honzon]] (佐渡始顕本尊)

[[File:Nose Myokenzan Nikken.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Statue of Nikken at Nose Myokenzan]]
'''Nikken''' (日乾, 1560 - December 6, 1635) was a Buddhist monk of the {{ill|Nichiren-shū|en|Nichiren-shū|WD=Q206611|lt=Nichiren-shū}} sect active during the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period}} to the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}. He was born in {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province}} and had the Dharma name "Kōjun" (孝順). He also bore the title "Jakushō-in" (寂照院). Nikken gained the patronage of {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|en|Tokugawa Yorinobu|WD=Q1045763|lt=Tokugawa Yorinobu}}, the lord of {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province}}'s {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain}}. With Yorinobu's support, Nikken played a significant role in the revival of [[Rennyō-ji Temple]] (蓮永寺) in {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province}}. == See Also == * [[Jōshō-ji Temple (Kyoto)]] * [[Sadoga Hajime Hokke Honzon]] (佐渡始顕本尊)
{{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日乾|version=103167267|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nikken]][[ja:日乾]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nikken]][[ja:日乾]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:日乾]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Nose Myokenzan Nikken.jpg|birth_date=1560|death_date={{death date|1635|12|6}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|WD=Q205662}}|religion={{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|WD=Q871371|1=Nichiren Buddhism}}}} {{Unreferenceds improvement|date=December 21, 2022, 10:46 (UTC)}}
[[File:Nose Myokenzan Nikken.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Statue of Nikken at Nose Myokenzan]]
'''Nikken''' (日乾, 1560 - December 6, 1635) was a Buddhist monk of the {{ill|Nichiren-shū|en|Nichiren-shū|WD=Q206611|lt=Nichiren-shū|1=Nichiren-shū}} sect active during the {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}} to the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. He was born in {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province}} and had the Dharma name "Kōjun" (孝順). He also bore the title "Jakushō-in" (寂照院). Nikken gained the patronage of {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|en|Tokugawa Yorinobu|WD=Q1045763|lt=Tokugawa Yorinobu|1=Tokugawa Yorinobu}}, the lord of {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}'s {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain|1=Kishū Domain}}. With Yorinobu's support, Nikken played a significant role in the revival of [[Rennyō-ji Temple]] (蓮永寺) in {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province|1=Suruga Province}}. == See Also == * [[Jōshō-ji Temple (Kyoto)]] * [[Sadoga Hajime Hokke Honzon]] (佐渡始顕本尊)
